steps.title: Subtract the second function
steps.content: Now compute $f(x)-g(x)$. The parentheses around $g(x)$ matter because subtracting $g(x)$ changes the signs of both terms in $18x-16$: $$f(x)-g(x)=(3x^2+17x-28)-(18x-16)$$ $$=3x^2+17x-28-18x+16$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Combine the $x$-terms and the constant terms: $$3x^2+17x-18x-28+16=3x^2-x-12$$ This simplified expression matches choice B. The answer is B.
